title: Add backpressure support
description: Flow control for fast producers / slow consumers at system boundaries. Keep rx3 core sync and lock-free, add observability and bounded queues at edges.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Flow control for fast producers / slow consumers.

### Problem
Current design: producer notifies all subscribers synchronously.
If subscriber is slow, producer blocks OR subscriber misses values.

### Lock-Free Strategies

**1. Drop strategies (simplest, fully lock-free)**
```rust
pub trait BackpressureExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn sample_latest(&self) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
}
```
- Use `ArcSwap<T>` to always hold latest value
- Consumer reads at its own pace, missing intermediate values
- Zero coordination overhead

**2. Bounded ring buffer (lock-free)**
```rust
use crossbeam::queue::ArrayQueue;
let buffer: Arc<ArrayQueue<T>> = Arc::new(ArrayQueue::new(capacity));
// Producer: buffer.push(v).ok(); // drops on full (force_push for drop-oldest)
// Consumer: buffer.pop()
```
- `ArrayQueue` is lock-free bounded MPMC queue
- Configurable drop behavior

**3. Lock-free channel backpressure (flume)**
```rust
let (tx, rx) = flume::bounded::<T>(capacity);
// tx.try_send() - returns error if full (drop-newest)
// tx.send_async() - backpressure via async/await
```

### Approach Options

**Option 1: Drop operators (recommended first)**
```rust
fn drop_oldest(&self, capacity: usize) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
fn drop_newest(&self, capacity: usize) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>  
fn sample_latest(&self) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>  // like throttle
```
- Use `crossbeam::ArrayQueue` for bounded buffer
- Implement as regular operators, no core changes

**Option 2: Async pull-based consumption**
```rust
pub trait AsyncConsumer<T> {
    async fn next(&mut self) -> Option<T>;  // pull-based
}
```
- Convert push to pull model using flume channel
- Ties into async/await integration (rx3-97n)

### Lock-Free Primitives to Use
- `ArcSwap<T>` - latest value only (sample_latest)
- `crossbeam::ArrayQueue<T>` - bounded MPMC ring buffer
- `flume` - lock-free async-aware channels

### Avoid
- `Mutex<VecDeque<T>>` - causes blocking
- `tokio::sync::mpsc` - uses internal locks
- Single-lock designs - create bottlenecks

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/operators/backpressure.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/operators/mod.rs`
- Add `crossbeam` dependency (lock-free queues)

### Related
- rx3-97n (async/await) - async consumption is form of backpressure
- rx3-ldf (buffer) - buffering is backpressure strategy

title: Completion signals for finite streams
notes:
## Implementation Notes

**Critical foundation** - many operators depend on this (concat, finalize, default_if_empty, last, first).

### Approach Options

**Option 1: Wrapper type**
```rust
enum StreamValue<T> {
    Value(T),
    Complete,
}
type FiniteCell<T> = Cell<StreamValue<T>, _>;
```

**Option 2: Separate completion channel**
```rust
pub struct FiniteCell<T, M> {
    cell: Cell<T, M>,
    completed: Arc<AtomicBool>,
    on_complete: Arc<DashMap<Uuid, Box<dyn Fn() + Send + Sync>>>,
}
```

**Option 3: Completable trait**
```rust
pub trait Completable {
    fn complete(&self);
    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool;
    fn on_complete(&self, f: impl Fn() + Send + Sync) -> Uuid;
}
```

### Key Decisions
- Does completion propagate automatically through derived cells?
- Can a cell emit after completion? (usually no in Rx)
- How do operators like `take(n)` signal completion after n items?

### Recommended Approach
Option 3 (Completable trait) is most flexible:
- Existing Cell stays unchanged
- Operators can implement Completable when they have finite semantics
- Compose completion propagation in derived cells

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/completable.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`
- Modify: `src/cell.rs` (impl Completable for Cell)
- Update: `take.rs`, `skip.rs` to signal completion

title: on_complete() TOCTOU race in cell.rs
description:
## Location
`rx3-lib/src/cell.rs:219-223`

## Problem
Race condition between checking `is_complete()` and inserting the callback:

```rust
fn on_complete(&self, callback: ...) -> SubscriptionGuard {
    if self.is_complete() {  // Check
        callback();
    }
    // GAP: complete() could run here, iterate callbacks, miss this one
    self.inner.on_complete.insert(id, Box::new(callback));  // Insert
}
```

If `complete()` fires between check and insert, the callback is never invoked.

## Fix Options
1. Use a lock around the check-and-insert
2. Check-after-insert pattern: insert first, then if complete, call callback and remove
3. Use `completed` as a generation counter - insert with expected gen, complete increments gen
close_reason: Fixed with insert-first-then-check pattern using atomic removal for exactly-once semantics

title: BoundedOutput - bounded channel for output subscribers
description: Subscriber that writes to bounded channel instead of direct callback. Allows output consumers to pull at their own pace. Decouples rx3 notification from slow I/O. Use with dedicated output threads for network writes.

title: Add distinct operator
description: Filter for global uniqueness across the stream lifetime. Unlike deduped (consecutive duplicates only), distinct tracks all seen values. May need HashSet internally.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Filter for global uniqueness (vs `deduped` which is consecutive only).

### Approach
```rust
pub trait DistinctExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn distinct(&self) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
    where T: Clone + Hash + Eq + Send + Sync + 'static
```

### Lock-Free Implementation

**Option 1: DashSet (sharded locks - acceptable)**
```rust
use dashmap::DashSet;
let seen: Arc<DashSet<T>> = Arc::new(DashSet::new());
// On emission:
if seen.insert(value.clone()) {
    c.notify(value.clone());  // insert returns true if new
}
```
Note: DashSet uses sharded internal locks, not a single Mutex. This provides good concurrent performance and is acceptable for this use case.

**Option 2: Lock-free set via crossbeam SkipMap**
```rust
use crossbeam_skiplist::SkipSet;
let seen: Arc<SkipSet<T>> = Arc::new(SkipSet::new());
// SkipSet is lock-free
if seen.insert(value.clone()).is_ok() {
    c.notify(value.clone());
}
```
Requires: `crossbeam-skiplist` crate

**Recommendation**: DashSet is acceptable - its sharded design avoids single-lock bottlenecks. For truly lock-free, use SkipSet.

### Memory Consideration
- Set grows unbounded over stream lifetime
- Consider variant: `distinct_until_capacity(n)` using bounded LRU
- Or `distinct_by(|v| key)` for custom key extraction

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/operators/distinct.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/operators/mod.rs`
- Use existing `dashmap` dependency

### Test Cases
- Distinct integers: [1,2,1,3,2] -> [1,2,3]
- Distinct with custom key
- Concurrent emission test

title: Excessive cloning in operator chains
description:
Performance issue: every operator clones values multiple times.

notify() clones:
```rust
self.inner.value.store(Arc::new(value.clone()));
```

Each operator clones again:
```rust
// map.rs - transform already returns new value
d.notify(transform(value));

// filter.rs - explicit clone
c.notify(value.clone());
```

Chain of 5 operators = 5+ clones of potentially large data.

Consider:
1. Use Arc<T> internally to share immutable values
2. Add zero-copy operators for reference-based transforms
3. Operator fusion to reduce intermediate allocations
4. Cow<T> for copy-on-write semantics
close_reason: Completed - refactored Signal to use Arc<T>, eliminating deep clones

title: Add finalize operator
description: Run cleanup callback on completion or unsubscribe. Useful for resource cleanup. Depends on completion signals (rx3-0tw).
notes:
## Implementation Notes

**Depends on: rx3-0tw (Completion signals)**

Run cleanup callback on completion or unsubscribe.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait FinalizeExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn finalize(&self, cleanup: impl Fn() + Send + Sync + 'static) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Pass through all values unchanged
- Register cleanup callback
- Call cleanup when:
  - Source completes (needs rx3-0tw)
  - All subscribers unsubscribe
  - Cell is disposed (needs rx3-7m0)
- Use `Arc<AtomicBool>` to ensure cleanup runs exactly once

### Blocked Until
- Completion signals (rx3-0tw) implemented
- Or implement partial version that only handles unsubscribe

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/finalize.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

### Test Cases
- Cleanup on completion
- Cleanup on unsubscribe
- Verify cleanup runs exactly once

title: Callback panics are unhandled - kills remaining subscribers
description:
cell.rs:147-152 - if one callback panics, rest don't get called:

```rust
self.inner.subscribers.iter().for_each(|subscriber| {
    (subscriber.callback)(&value);  // If this panics...
});
// All remaining subscribers skipped\!
```

Also:
- Mutex poisoning risk: cell.rs:81,114,139 use .unwrap() on locks
- If callback panics while lock held, Mutex is poisoned
- All subsequent .lock() calls will panic

Need:
1. Wrap callback execution in catch_unwind
2. Handle PoisonError gracefully
3. Consider logging/reporting panicked callbacks
close_reason: Fixed in notify() - collect callbacks first, wrap in catch_unwind

title: Add skip_while operator
description: Skip emissions while predicate returns true, then emit all subsequent values. Complement to take_while (rx3-mtd).
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Skip emissions while predicate returns true, then emit all after.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait SkipWhileExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn skip_while(&self, predicate: impl Fn(&T) -> bool + Send + Sync + 'static) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Use `Arc<AtomicBool>` to track "skipping" state
- Start in skipping=true state
- On emission: if skipping and predicate(value), skip
- Once predicate returns false, set skipping=false permanently
- All subsequent values pass through

### Pattern
Similar to `take.rs` which uses AtomicUsize for state.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/skip_while.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

### Test Cases
- skip_while(|x| x < 5) on [1,2,3,6,4,7] -> [6,4,7]
- Verify values after predicate fails all pass through
- Edge case: predicate never fails (empty output)

title: Add start_with operator
description: Prepend initial value(s) to a stream. Useful for providing default/seed values before the first emission.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Follow the extension trait pattern in `src/traits/`.

### Approach
`start_with` prepends initial value(s) before source emissions:
```rust
pub trait StartWithExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn start_with(&self, value: T) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
    fn start_with_iter(&self, values: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Create derived cell with the start_with value as initial
- Immediately notify subscribers with start_with value(s)
- Then watch source and forward subsequent emissions
- Use `AtomicBool` to skip first watch callback (already handled by initial)

### Consideration
Since `watch()` calls callback immediately with current value, need to handle:
- Option 1: Use a flag to emit start_with value before first watch callback
- Option 2: Emit start_with values, then set up watch normally

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/start_with.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

title: Add zip operator
description: Pair emissions 1:1 from two sources. Unlike combine (which fires on any change), zip waits for both sources to emit before pairing. Requires same or compatible cadence between sources.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Follow the extension trait pattern in `src/traits/`.

### Approach
`zip` pairs emissions 1:1, requiring buffering until both sources have emitted:
```rust
pub trait ZipExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn zip<U, M>(&self, other: &Cell<U, M>) -> Cell<(T, U), CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Use two `Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<T>>>` buffers (one per source)
- On each emission, push to buffer, check if other buffer has item
- If both have items, pop from both and emit tuple
- Watch both sources independently

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/zip.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs` (add export)

### Test Cases
- Zip two cells with same emission rate
- Zip cells with different rates (verify buffering)
- Verify memory doesn't grow unbounded with slow consumer

title: Add first operator
Cell disposal mechanism
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Mechanism to clean up cell subscriptions and resources.

### Current State
- `SubscriptionGuard` provides RAII unsubscribe on drop
- But cells themselves don't have cleanup mechanism
- Derived cells hold Arc refs to parents (memory leak potential)

### Approach Options

**Option 1: Disposable trait**
```rust
pub trait Disposable {
    fn dispose(&self);
    fn is_disposed(&self) -> bool;
}
```
- Dispose clears subscribers, marks cell as dead
- Subsequent `watch()` calls are no-ops
- Derived cells auto-dispose when all parents disposed

**Option 2: Weak references for deps**
```rust
dependencies: Arc<Vec<Weak<dyn DepNode>>>
```
- Parents can be dropped naturally
- Derived cells detect dead parents and stop

**Option 3: Explicit lifecycle**
```rust
pub struct CellHandle<T> { ... }  // Strong ref
pub struct CellRef<T> { ... }     // Weak ref for derived cells
```

### Considerations
- Current design: `watch()` holds Arc to self via callback closure
- This creates strong reference cycles
- May need to redesign subscription storage

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/disposable.rs`
- Modify: `src/cell.rs` (add disposal state)
- Modify: All operators to check disposal state

### Related
- Works with completion signals (rx3-0tw) - complete then dispose

title: Add sample operator
description: Emit the latest value from source whenever a sampler/notifier fires. Useful for rate-limiting based on external signals.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Emit latest value from source when sampler/notifier fires.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait SampleExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn sample<U, M>(&self, sampler: &Cell<U, M>) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Watch source: store latest value in `Arc<ArcSwap<T>>`
- Watch sampler: on sampler emission, emit current stored value
- Don't emit on source changes (that's combine_latest)
- Only emit when sampler fires

### Alternative: sample_time
```rust
fn sample_time(&self, interval: Duration) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```
- Use internal interval timer as sampler

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/sample.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

### Test Cases
- Sample slow source with fast sampler
- Sample fast source with slow sampler
- Verify only sampler triggers emission

title: switch_map has duplicate generation counters
description:
## Location
`rx3-lib/src/traits/operators/switch_map.rs`

## Problem
Two separate generation counters exist:
- `generation` (line 18) - used for value emission
- `current_gen` (line 67) - used for completion tracking

Both are incremented in separate `self.subscribe()` callbacks (lines 38, 94), which race against each other and can get out of sync.

When outer emits:
1. `outer_guard` callback increments `generation`
2. `switch_tracker` callback increments `current_gen`

These run as separate subscription callbacks and may interleave with inner emissions/completions.

## Fix
Unify into a single generation counter used for both emission gating and completion tracking. Only subscribe to outer once.
close_reason: Unified to single generation counter and single outer subscription

title: with_latest_from operator
description: When source emits, combine with latest from another cell: a.with_latest_from(&b, |a, b| (a, b)). Only emits when a changes, using latest b.

title: Backpressure detection API
description: Add methods to Cell: is_backed_up() -> bool (heuristic based on last notify time), try_set_timeout(value, Duration) -> Result (reject if system overloaded or notify too slow). Provides visibility without changing sync core.

title: Subscription lifecycle - auto-cleanup on derived cell drop
close_reason: Implemented subscription cleanup via on_drop and subscribe_to_parent helper. All operators now unsubscribe when derived cells are dropped.

title: buffer_count operator
description: Collect N values into a Vec before emitting: source.buffer_count(3) emits Vec<T> with 3 elements at a time.

title: Async/await integration
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Enable `async/await` usage with rx3 cells.

### Lock-Free Channel Options

**Preferred: flume (lock-free, bounded/unbounded)**
```rust
use flume::{bounded, unbounded};
let (tx, rx) = bounded::<T>(16);  // Lock-free bounded channel
```
- `flume` is fully lock-free for both send and receive
- Supports async and sync APIs

**Alternative: crossbeam-channel**
```rust
use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, select};
```
- Lock-free multi-producer multi-consumer
- Sync only (wrap with async adapter)

**Avoid: tokio::sync::mpsc**
- Uses internal Mutex, not lock-free
- Only use if tokio runtime is already required

### Approach Options

**Option 1: Stream adapter with flume**
```rust
impl<T, M> Cell<T, M> {
    pub fn to_stream(&self) -> impl Stream<Item = T> {
        let (tx, rx) = flume::unbounded();
        self.subscribe(move |v| { let _ = tx.send(v.clone()); });
        rx.into_stream()  // flume provides async Stream
    }
}
```

**Option 2: Async next value**
```rust
pub async fn next(&self) -> T  // Wait for next emission
```
- Use `tokio::sync::Notify` (uses atomic wait, acceptable)
- Or use `event-listener` crate (fully lock-free)

**Option 3: AsyncWatchable trait**
```rust
pub trait AsyncWatchable<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn watch_async(&self) -> impl Stream<Item = T>;
}
```

### Dependencies
- Add `flume` as optional dependency (lock-free)
- Feature flag: `async`

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/async_support.rs` or `src/stream.rs`
- Modify: `Cargo.toml` (add flume as optional dep)
- Modify: `src/lib.rs` (feature-gated export)

### Lock-Free Rationale
- flume provides wait-free sends on unbounded channels
- Avoids Mutex contention in high-throughput scenarios
- Composable with other lock-free primitives in rx3

title: Add take_until operator
Simple operators: propagate source completion (map, filter, tap, skip, deduped, scan, pairwise, delay, parallel)
Add timeout operator
start_with operator
description: Prepend an initial value before source values: source.start_with(default). Useful for ensuring a known starting state.
close_reason: Not needed - Cells always have a current value

title: Add default_if_empty operator
description: Emit a default value if the stream completes without emitting anything. Depends on completion signals (rx3-0tw).
notes:
## Implementation Notes

**Depends on: rx3-0tw (Completion signals)**

Emit default value if stream completes without emitting.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait DefaultIfEmptyExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn default_if_empty(&self, default: T) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Track whether source has emitted: `Arc<AtomicBool>`
- On source emission: set has_emitted=true, forward value
- On source completion: if !has_emitted, emit default
- Pass through completion signal after

### Blocked Until
- Completion signals (rx3-0tw) implemented

### Alternative: default_value for initial
Current `Cell::new(initial)` already provides initial value.
This operator is specifically for finite streams that may complete empty.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/default_if_empty.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

title: switch_map: complete when outer and current inner complete
Error propagation and handling
close_reason: Implemented Result<T,E> wrapper approach with try_map, map_ok, map_err, catch_error, unwrap_or, unwrap_or_else operators

title: BoundedInput - bounded channel wrapper at ingestion
description: Wrapper type for bounded channels at input boundaries. Configurable overflow policy: Block, DropOldest, DropNewest, Error. Use crossbeam::ArrayQueue for lock-free bounded buffer. Reports backpressure events via metrics.

title: Add concat operator
description: Sequential stream composition - subscribe to second source after first completes. Depends on completion signals (rx3-0tw).
notes:
## Implementation Notes

**Depends on: rx3-0tw (Completion signals)**

### Approach
`concat` subscribes to second source only after first completes:
```rust
pub trait ConcatExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn concat<M>(&self, other: &Cell<T, M>) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Requires completion signaling mechanism from rx3-0tw
- Watch first source, forward emissions
- On first source completion, subscribe to second source
- Use `AtomicBool` to track completion state

### Blocked Until
- Completion signals (rx3-0tw) must be implemented first
- Need to define how cells signal completion (Option wrapper? Separate trait?)

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/concat.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

title: from_iter_with_delay panics on empty iterator
description:
from_iter.rs:12 panics if iterator is empty:

```rust
let first = iter.next().expect("from_iter_with_delay requires at least one element");
```

Should return Result or Option instead of panicking.

Fix: Return Result<Cell, Error> or handle empty case gracefully.

title: take_while: complete when predicate becomes false
Add merge operator
description: Combine multiple streams of the same type T into one stream. All sources must have the same inner type. Emits whenever any source emits.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Follow the extension trait pattern in `src/traits/`.

### Approach
`merge` combines N sources of same type T into one stream:
```rust
pub trait MergeExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn merge<M>(&self, other: &Cell<T, M>) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

Consider also a variadic version via macro or slice input.

### Key Implementation Details
- Create derived cell, watch all sources
- On any emission, forward directly to derived cell
- No buffering needed (unlike zip)
- Track all parents in deps vec for dependency graph

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/merge.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs` (add export)

### Test Cases
- Merge two cells, verify both emissions come through
- Merge cells with different emission rates
- Verify dependency tracking with multiple parents

title: switch_map completion has TOCTOU race
close_reason: Fixed with lock-free CAS loops on packed AtomicU64 (gen + inner_complete + outer_complete)

title: Completion TOCTOU race in merge/merge_map
description:
## Locations
- `rx3-lib/src/traits/operators/merge.rs:50-67`
- `rx3-lib/src/traits/operators/merge_map.rs:62-68, 94-100`

## Problem
Classic TOCTOU on completion tracking. Pattern:

```rust
// Thread A (source 1 completes)
sc.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
if oc.load(Ordering::SeqCst) { complete() }  // sees false

// Thread B (source 2 completes) - simultaneous
oc.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
if sc.load(Ordering::SeqCst) { complete() }  // may see false due to race
```

If both sources complete simultaneously, neither may see the other's store.

## Fix Options
1. Use a single `AtomicU8` with bit flags and compare-exchange
2. Use `fetch_or` on a combined state and check result
3. Use a Mutex for the completion check (heavier but simpler)
close_reason: merge.rs refactored to use fetch_or; merge_map analyzed and confirmed correct with SeqCst (total order guarantees at least one sees the other's store)

title: merge/join: complete when both sources complete
Fix debounce/throttle race conditions
close_reason: Not a bug - both implementations are correct. Debounce generation check correctly detects superseded values. Throttle can_emit is already false during spawn window.

title: Cancellable constructors - interval() and from_iter_with_delay() leak threads
close_reason: Fixed by using weak references - threads exit when cell is dropped

title: state_transition operator
notify() is not atomic - subscribers can change mid-iteration
close_reason: Fixed in notify() - collect callbacks first, wrap in catch_unwind

title: sample operator
description: Sample the source whenever a notifier emits: source.sample(&ticker). Emits the latest value from source when ticker fires.

title: zip: complete when either source completes
throttle: emit pending value and complete on source completion
Add buffer operator
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Similar to window but emits collected values differently.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait BufferExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn buffer_count(&self, count: usize) -> Cell<Vec<T>, CellImmutable>
    fn buffer_time(&self, duration: Duration) -> Cell<Vec<T>, CellImmutable>
}
```

### Lock-Free Implementation

Use same patterns as window operator (rx3-wm5):

**buffer_count (ArcSwap copy-on-write):**
```rust
let buffer = Arc::new(ArcSwap::from_pointee(Vec::with_capacity(count)));
// On emission: clone, push, swap atomically
```

**buffer_time (crossbeam SegQueue):**
```rust
use crossbeam::queue::SegQueue;
let queue: Arc<SegQueue<T>> = Arc::new(SegQueue::new());
// Timer drains queue into Vec and emits
```

### Lock-Free Rationale
- Consistent with codebase pattern (ArcSwap, atomics)
- Avoid Mutex for better composability in reactive chains
- crossbeam provides battle-tested lock-free primitives

### Difference from Window
In classic Rx:
- `buffer` collects into Vec/array
- `window` emits nested Observables

For rx3, both emit `Vec<T>` since we dont have nested observables.
Consider making buffer an alias for window or vice versa.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/operators/buffer.rs` (or alias to window)
- Modify: `src/traits/operators/mod.rs`

### Dependencies
- `crossbeam` for SegQueue (lock-free queue)

title: Add take_while operator
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Emit values while predicate returns true, then complete.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait TakeWhileExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn take_while(&self, predicate: impl Fn(&T) -> bool + Send + Sync + 'static) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Use `Arc<AtomicBool>` to track "taking" state (starts true)
- On emission: if taking and predicate(value), emit
- Once predicate returns false, set taking=false
- Stop emitting, optionally signal completion (needs rx3-0tw)

### Pattern
Mirror of skip_while:
- skip_while: skip until predicate fails, then pass all
- take_while: pass until predicate fails, then skip all

### Without Completion Signals
Can implement now - just stops emitting.
Add completion signal support later when rx3-0tw done.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/take_while.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

### Test Cases
- take_while(|x| x < 5) on [1,2,3,6,4] -> [1,2,3]
- Verify no emissions after predicate fails
- Edge case: first value fails predicate

title: debounce: emit pending value and complete on source completion
audit operator
description: Like throttle but emits the LAST value in the window instead of first: source.audit(Duration::from_millis(100)). Silences during window, then emits most recent.

title: distinct_until_changed_by operator
description: Like deduped() but takes a custom comparator closure: .distinct_until_changed_by(|a, b| a.id == b.id). Only emits when comparator returns false.

title: Add concurrency and stress tests
description:
Current tests are single-threaded and don't cover edge cases:

Missing tests:
- Concurrent subscribers (100s of simultaneous watchers)
- Callbacks that modify the cell they're watching
- Callbacks that panic
- Unsubscribe during notification
- Watching a cell from within a watcher callback
- Memory leak detection (create/drop 1000s of cells)
- Operator chains of 10+ depth
- combine!() with 10+ cells
- from_iter_with_delay with empty iterator

Need:
1. Stress tests with thread pools
2. Memory benchmarks (track allocations)
3. Loom or similar for concurrency testing
4. Property-based tests for invariants

title: Add share/replay multicasting
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Enable multiple subscribers to share a single subscription to source.

### Approach

**share()** - Hot multicast:
```rust
pub trait ShareExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn share(&self) -> SharedCell<T>
}
```
- Single subscription to source
- Multiple subscribers to SharedCell
- Late subscribers miss past emissions

**replay(n)** - Replay buffer:
```rust
pub trait ReplayExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn replay(&self, buffer_size: usize) -> ReplayCell<T>
}
```
- Buffer last N emissions
- Late subscribers receive buffered values immediately

### Key Implementation Details
- `SharedCell`: wraps source, manages refcount of subscribers
- Subscribe to source on first subscriber
- Unsubscribe from source when last subscriber leaves
- `ReplayCell`: add `Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<T>>>` ring buffer

### Consideration
Current Cell already multicasts (DashMap of subscribers).
This is more about:
1. Lazy subscription (don't subscribe until someone watches)
2. Replay buffer for late subscribers
3. Reference counting for cleanup

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/shared_cell.rs` or `src/multicasting.rs`
- Create: `src/traits/share.rs`
- Create: `src/traits/replay.rs`
close_reason: Redundant - cells are already shared (hot) and replay current value on subscribe

title: merge_map: complete when outer and all inner cells complete
Add last operator
notes:
## Implementation Notes

**Depends on: rx3-0tw (Completion signals)**

Emit only the last value before completion.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait LastExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn last(&self) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
}
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Store latest value in `Arc<ArcSwap<Option<T>>>`
- On each emission, update stored value (don't emit yet)
- On completion, emit stored value (if any)
- Requires completion signal from rx3-0tw

### Alternative: last_or
```rust
fn last_or(&self, default: T) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
```
- Emit default if stream completes without values

### Blocked Until
- Completion signals (rx3-0tw) - need to know when stream ends

### Without Completion (Limited Use)
For infinite streams, `last` doesn't make sense.
Could implement `last_n(count)` that tracks last N values.

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/last.rs`
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`

title: Result operators: propagate source completion (try_map, map_ok, map_err, catch_error, unwrap_or)
Add combine_latest operator
description: Operator form of the combine! macro. Emits tuple of latest values whenever any source emits. Alternative API to the macro for cases where operator chaining is preferred.
notes:
## Implementation Notes

Operator form of the existing `combine!` macro.

### Approach
`combine_latest` emits tuple when any source changes:
```rust
pub trait CombineLatestExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn combine_latest<U, M>(&self, other: &Cell<U, M>) -> Cell<(T, U), CellImmutable>
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Essentially wraps the `combine!` macro as a method
- Can delegate to: `combine!((self, other), |a, b| (a.clone(), b.clone()))`
- Similar to existing `join` implementation in `src/traits/join.rs`

### Consideration
- Is this redundant with `join`? 
- `join` already does exactly this
- Consider: rename `join` to `combine_latest`? Or keep both for RxJS familiarity?

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/combine_latest.rs` (or just alias join)
- Modify: `src/traits/mod.rs`
close_reason: Redundant - join() already implements combine_latest semantics

title: CellMetrics - observability for cell performance
description: Add metrics tracking to Cell: notify_count, notify_time_ns, slowest_subscriber_ns, subscriber_count. Use atomics for lock-free updates. Enables detection of slow subscribers and system health monitoring.

title: Unify subscription APIs - three conflicting patterns
description:
Currently three ways to subscribe with different semantics:

1. watch() -> Uuid - manual cleanup, easy to leak
2. subscribe() -> SubscriptionGuard - RAII, but separate trait
3. combine!() - creates internal watchers with no cleanup handle

Problems:
- Users don't know which to use
- watch() is a footgun (ignoring returned Uuid leaks)
- combine!() subscriptions can't be cancelled

Proposal:
- Deprecate raw watch() or make it return SubscriptionGuard
- Make subscribe() the primary API
- Add subscription handle to combine!() result
close_reason: Unified watch/subscribe into single Watchable::subscribe method

title: Add window operator
Add retry operator
notes:
## Implementation Notes

**Depends on: rx3-cgz (Error propagation)**

Retry failed operations.

### Approach
```rust
pub trait RetryExt<T>: Watchable<T> {
    fn retry(&self, max_attempts: usize) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
    fn retry_when<F>(&self, notifier: F) -> Cell<T, CellImmutable>
    where F: Fn(Error) -> bool  // return true to retry
}
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Requires error propagation mechanism (rx3-cgz)
- On error, resubscribe to source
- Track attempt count with AtomicUsize
- After max_attempts, propagate error

### Consideration
Current cells don't have error concept - values are just T.
Need to decide:
- `Cell<Result<T, E>>` pattern?
- Separate error channel?
- Wrapper type `Fallible<T>`?

### Blocked Until
- Error propagation (rx3-cgz) design decided

### Files to Create/Modify
- Create: `src/traits/retry.rs`
- Depends on error handling infrastructure

title: Slow subscriber detection and alerting
description: Track per-subscriber timing. Configurable slow_subscriber_threshold. Emit warning/callback when subscriber exceeds budget. Optional: auto-unsubscribe pathologically slow subscribers. Helps identify bottlenecks in subscriber chain.

title: distinct operator
description: Only emit values that have never been seen before. Maintains a HashSet of seen values. Requires T: Hash + Eq.
